title: Self-signed certificate error with Jira integration
symptom: When attempting to connect to Jira using a custom SSL certificate, n8n throws a self-signed certificate error. This indicates that the certificate chain is not trusted.
root_cause: The self-signed certificate error occurs because n8n does not recognize the certificate chain provided. This typically happens when the certificate does not include the necessary root or intermediate CA certificates that establish trust.
fix_steps: Extract the correct CA that signed the Jira certificate
Combine the root and intermediate certificates into a single PEM file
Ensure the combined PEM file has the correct permissions and is mounted in the n8n container.
agent_instruction: Provide a single PEM file containing the trusted CA certificates for Jira.
